input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8
# Or for .NET 9:
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover current MAUI workload name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows targeting requires the Windows SDK and WinUI/UWP build support. The simplest path:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, for a minimal CLI-only install with required components:
# First, discover available component IDs:
winget show Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then install with workloads:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --quiet
```

If you prefer the **command-line bootstrapper**:

```powershell
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vs_community.exe" -OutFile vs_community.exe

.\vs_community.exe --wait --add `
  Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --includeRecommended --quiet
```

## 4. Install Android SDK & Emulator

MAUI's workload install handles most of this, but to verify/manage manually:

```powershell
# Check what Android components the MAUI workload installed
dotnet workload list

# The Android SDK is typically at:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
# Or under the MAUI/VS path:
$androidSdk = "$env:PROGRAMFILES(x86)\Android\android-sdk"

# Verify sdkmanager exists
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list
```

If you need to install the Android SDK **manually**:

```powershell
# Download command-line tools
$url = "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-11076708_latest.zip"
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $url -OutFile cmdline-tools.zip
Expand-Archive cmdline-tools.zip -DestinationPath "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools"

# Rename to expected structure
Rename-Item "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\cmdline-tools" "latest"

# Set environment variable
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# Dynamically discover and install the latest platform + build tools
$sdkmgr = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# List available packages to find latest versions
& $sdkmgr --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -Last 5
& $sdkmgr --list | Select-String "build-tools;" | Select-Object -Last 5
& $sdkmgr --list | Select-String "system-images.*google_apis;x86_64"

# Install (substitute discovered versions)
& $sdkmgr "platform-tools" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" `
           "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --verbose
& $sdkmgr --licenses  # Accept all licenses
```

## 5. Set Up Android Emulator

```powershell
# Create an AVD (Android Virtual Device)
$avdmgr = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

# List available device definitions
& $avdmgr list device

# Create emulator
& $avdmgr create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" -d "pixel"

# Launch (use -no-window for headless CI)
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## 6. Enable Windows Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d "1"
```

## 7. Verify Everything Works

```powershell
# Create a new MAUI project
dotnet new maui -n TestMauiApp
cd TestMauiApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
# (or net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0 for .NET 9)

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (ensure emulator is started)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Install -t:Run
```

## 8. Dynamic Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

```powershell
# Discover latest .NET SDK available via winget
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Discover installed target frameworks
dotnet --list-sdks

# Discover available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Discover Android target framework version your SDK expects
dotnet new maui -n Temp --dry-run 2>$null
Select-String "net\d+-android" Temp/*.csproj

# Discover latest Windows SDK installed
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" |
  Sort-Object Name | Select-Object -Last 1
```

---

**Key gotcha**: The `net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0` TFM must match an installed Windows SDK version. Use the last command above to find yours and substitute accordingly.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They want PowerShell commands and dynamic version discovery. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
